questions.question: Articles fastened with pins if detected at office of booking will be...
questions.explanation: According to Post Office guidelines, articles fastened with pins are not permitted and are returned to the sender immediately to prevent injury to postal staff and damage to other mail.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Trial Card...
questions.explanation: The Trial Card is denoted by the form number M 26(a) in the postal manual.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Discount for walk-in customers under Speed Post begins with minimum daily revenue of...
questions.explanation: Speed Post offers a discount structure for walk-in customers that begins when the minimum daily revenue reaches Rs 2,000.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Any change of location of franking machine, except in cases of repairs, shall be notified to the...
questions.explanation: To maintain security and proper auditing, any change in the location of a franking machine must be notified to both the Office of posting and the Head of the Division.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: What is the maximum age limit for enrolment of SB account holder under PMJJBY?
questions.explanation: The Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana (PMJJBY) is available to people in the age group of 18 to 50 years having a bank account.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Which bag will be booked and delivered on all working days including Sundays and holidays?
questions.explanation: Camp bags are special bags meant for high dignitaries on tour and are booked and delivered on all days, including Sundays and holidays.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: In case BNPL customer fails to make the payments of the bills by due date, what will be the rate of penalty?
questions.explanation: For Book Now Pay Later (BNPL) customers, failure to pay bills by the due date attracts a penal interest at the rate of 12% per annum.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Within how many days should the renter of a Post Box inform the Postmaster about change of his address?
questions.explanation: If the renter of a Post Box changes their business or residential address, they must inform the Postmaster within 7 days.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: GDS employees can enroll as Atal Pension Yojana subscribers...

options.A: If eligible as per age criteria
options.B: Shall not be eligible for Govt contribution
options.C: In addition to the membership of SDBS

questions.explanation: Gramin Dak Sevaks (GDS) can enroll in APY if they meet the age criteria, they will not receive the co-contribution from the Government since they are already covered under schemes like SDBS, and it can be in addition to SDBS.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Following bags are not treated as due bags...
questions.explanation: Cash bags are not considered 'due bags' because they are only dispatched when there is an actual remittance of cash, unlike routine bags.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Post Offices will not accept payment of postage on postal article in the shape of...
questions.explanation: Revenue stamps are intended for tax purposes on documents and receipts, not for prepaying postal charges.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Following orders are issued by Superintendent, RMS prescribing changes in sorting lists...
questions.explanation: 'A orders' are issued by the Superintendent of RMS to prescribe changes in sorting lists and routing of mails.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: The impressions of the franking machines recorded on the postal article should...
questions.explanation: Franking machine impressions must meet specific standards: they must be clear, distinct, stamped in bright red ink, and must not overlap.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Which is the nodal agency for implementation and monitoring of NPS?
questions.explanation: The Pension Fund Regulatory and Development Authority (PFRDA) is the statutory body regulating and monitoring the National Pension System (NPS).
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: The special registration envelopes provided by the post office can be used for transmission of...
questions.explanation: Special registration envelopes sold by the Post Office come with pre-paid postage specifically for registered postal articles.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: While booking International Speed Post articles below the value of SDR 300, which form is used?
questions.explanation: The CN22 customs declaration form is used for international postal articles weighing less than 2 kg and valued below SDR 300.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Post Office Savings Bank account will be treated as silent, if no deposits/withdrawals take place in an account during continuous ___ financial years.
questions.explanation: A Post Office Savings Account becomes silent or dormant if there are no transactions (deposit or withdrawal) for three consecutive financial years.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: The weight of unregistered domestic parcel should not exceed...
questions.explanation: The maximum permissible weight for an unregistered domestic parcel is 4 kilograms.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Premature closure of MIS account is not allowed before the expiry of ___ from the date of opening of such account.
questions.explanation: A Monthly Income Scheme (MIS) account cannot be closed prematurely before the completion of one year from the date of opening.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: In the case of articles addressed to foreign countries, the address should be written in...
questions.explanation: According to Universal Postal Union (UPU) guidelines, international mail addresses must be written in Roman letters and Arabic numerals for universal readability.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: The general maximum weight of International Speed Post (EMS) article is...
questions.explanation: The general maximum weight limit for booking an International Speed Post (EMS) article is 35 kg, subject to destination country regulations.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Which of the following is an office of exchange which closes bags for and receives bags from foreign countries in India?
questions.explanation: A Foreign Post Office acts as the Office of Exchange for processing and handling mail bags dispatched to and received from foreign countries.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Amount of deposit of a Post Box will not be refunded by the Postmaster at the time of surrender to the renter, if...

options.A: Post Box is not in use
options.B: Articles not collected from Post Box
options.C: Lock and key of Post Box is not working

questions.explanation: The deposit is forfeited if the lock and key are not returned in good working condition when the Post Box is surrendered.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Andhra Pradesh Postal Circle is under the administrative control of...
questions.explanation: Every Postal Circle in India is headed by a Chief Postmaster General (CPMG) who exercises administrative control over it.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: What is meant by 'CRC' in RMS Division?
questions.explanation: In Railway Mail Service (RMS) terminology, CRC stands for Computerised Registration Centre, where registered mail is processed digitally.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: What is the minimum amount of deposit to open an account under Senior Citizen Savings Scheme?
questions.explanation: The minimum deposit required to open a Senior Citizen Savings Scheme (SCSS) account is Rs 1,000, and deposits must be in multiples of 1,000.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Which of the following is a stationery office of the Railway Mail Service?
questions.explanation: Record Offices, Sub Record Offices, and Head Record Offices all function as stationary offices of the Railway Mail Service for supplying forms, bags, and maintaining records.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: What is the maximum age at entry for opening of Gram Priya policy?
questions.explanation: The maximum age for entry into the Gram Priya (10 Year Rural Postal Life Insurance) policy is 45 years.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Department of Posts has launched a scholarship scheme called Deen Dayal Sparsh Yojana to award children in class category of...
questions.explanation: The Deen Dayal SPARSH Yojana is a scholarship scheme specifically to promote philately among children studying in Standards VI to IX.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: A bus travels for 6 hours and covers a distance at the speed of 60 km per hour. If it travels at the speed of 80 km per hour, how much time will the bus take to cover the same distance?
questions.explanation: Distance = Speed × Time = 60 km/h × 6 h = 360 km. New Time = Distance / New Speed = 360 / 80 = 4.5 hours (4 1/2 hours).
questions.subject: Numerical Ability

questions.question: Ayyappa sold an article for Rs 1605/- and got 7% profit. What was its cost price?
questions.explanation: Cost Price (CP) = Selling Price / (1 + Profit%). CP = 1605 / 1.07 = Rs 1500.
questions.subject: Numerical Ability

questions.question: Which Indian won the Booker Prize of 2025 for 'Heart Lamp'?
questions.explanation: According to the official exam key, Banu Mushtaq is the recognized answer for this contemporary general knowledge question.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Who was the first Indian to score a century in Women's T20 International Cricket?
questions.explanation: Harmanpreet Kaur became the first Indian woman to score a century in T20 Internationals against New Zealand in November 2018.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: The longest bone in human body is located in...
questions.explanation: The femur, which is the longest and strongest bone in the human body, is located in the thigh.
questions.subject: Science

questions.question: Which is the longest highway in India?
questions.explanation: National Highway 44 (NH 44) is the longest-running major north-south National Highway in India, running from Srinagar to Kanyakumari.
questions.subject: Geography

questions.question: Who is the first Indian to receive Bharat Ratna Award?
questions.explanation: Dr. Sarvepalli Radhakrishnan, along with C. Rajagopalachari and C.V. Raman, was the first recipient of the Bharat Ratna in 1954.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: India shares longest boundary with...
questions.explanation: India shares its longest international land border with Bangladesh, stretching approximately 4,096 kilometers.
questions.subject: Geography

questions.question: When is the World Post Day celebrated?
questions.explanation: World Post Day is celebrated globally on October 9 to mark the anniversary of the establishment of the Universal Postal Union in 1874.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Who ordered partition of Bengal creating East Bengal and Assam out of the rest of Bengal in 1905?
questions.explanation: The Partition of Bengal in 1905 was ordered by Lord Curzon, the then Viceroy of India.
questions.subject: History

questions.question: 888 is divisible by 8. Also it is exactly divisible by...
questions.explanation: 888 is even (divisible by 2). Its sum of digits is 24 (divisible by 3). Its last two digits are 88 (divisible by 4). Since it is divisible by both 2 and 3, it is also divisible by 6.
questions.subject: Numerical Ability

questions.question: Choose the correct option to complete the statement: 'All the multiples of 8 are also the ...'
questions.explanation: Any number that is a multiple of 8 must inherently be a multiple of the factors of 8, which are 1, 2, and 4.
questions.subject: Numerical Ability

questions.question: A Finance Company sold shares worth Rs. 9540. If each share costs Rs. 45, find the total number of shares sold.
questions.explanation: Total number of shares = Total value / Cost per share = 9540 / 45 = 212.
questions.subject: Numerical Ability

questions.question: The last Mughal Emperor Bahadur Shah II used to write 'Shairis' in the pen name of...
questions.explanation: Bahadur Shah II was an accomplished Urdu poet and used the nom de plume (takhallus) 'Zafar', meaning victory.
questions.subject: History

questions.question: The Civil Disobedience Movement was started by Mahatma Gandhi on...
questions.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi launched the Civil Disobedience Movement with the historic Dandi March, which began on 12th March 1930.
questions.subject: History

questions.question: There are 600 students in a school in which 58% are boys. The difference of the number of boys and girls is...
questions.explanation: Boys = 58%. Girls = 100% - 58% = 42%. Difference in percentage = 58% - 42% = 16%. The difference in numbers is 16% of 600 = 96.
questions.subject: Numerical Ability

questions.question: A television set was purchased for Rs. 30,000. If it was sold for Rs. 28,500, what was the percentage of loss?
questions.explanation: Loss = Cost Price - Selling Price = 30,000 - 28,500 = 1,500. Loss Percentage = (Loss / Cost Price) × 100 = (1,500 / 30,000) × 100 = 5%.
questions.subject: Numerical Ability

questions.question: Ramanna borrowed a loan of Rs. 5400 from a bank at the rate of 10 paise per 10 rupees per month. Calculate the interest for one year.
questions.explanation: Rate per month = 10 paise / 10 rupees = 0.10 / 10 = 1% per month. For 1 year (12 months), interest = Principal × Rate × Time = 5400 × (1/100) × 12 = Rs 648.
questions.subject: Numerical Ability

questions.question: Find the average of 27, 36, 45, 54, 63.
questions.explanation: Since the numbers are in an arithmetic progression with a common difference of 9, the average is simply the middle number, which is 45. Alternatively: (27+36+45+54+63)/5 = 225/5 = 45.
questions.subject: Numerical Ability

questions.question: In 8th standard examination, average of Manasi's marks in five subjects, other than Mathematics, was 62. The average of marks in six subjects, including Mathematics, was 65. How many marks did she score in Mathematics?
questions.explanation: Total marks in 5 subjects = 62 × 5 = 310. Total marks in 6 subjects = 65 × 6 = 390. Marks in Mathematics = 390 - 310 = 80.
questions.subject: Numerical Ability
